summary:
The document structure is coherent: iteration-2 facts, followed by iteration-3 facts, then the unchanged synthesis sections with pointers updated to disambiguate cross-iteration references.

I re-explored the repository to ground this iteration's two critic findings in concrete code facts. For F1 (resume vs. Done ambiguity), I grepped the whole `app/` tree and confirmed no "resume" concept exists anywhere in code — no `paused` status on `Phase`/`Pipeline`, and the only two existing paths that take a held phase back into the automatic loop (`Approve`, `SendBack`) are always bundled with a triggering action, never a bare "un-pause" primitive — so R5/R26's "resumes" language has nothing to anchor to today. For F2 (in-flight display during a single-step menu re-run), I found a concrete existing precedent: the step card (`_step_card.html.erb`) hides its "Re-run" link entirely for a run's whole `ready/claimed/running` lifetime and instead shows a live `aria-live` progress line, driven by Turbo Stream broadcasts off `StepRun.state` — a reusable model for what Explore/Clarifying-Questions could do to the paused menu, distinct from the simpler `turbo_submits_with` button-label pattern used on the existing "Send answers" form. I added a new "Facts sharpening the iteration-3 critic feedback" section with these findings, cross-referenced them into the existing open-questions list (adding item 11 and annotating items 2, 6, and 8), and disambiguated the F1/F2 labels from iteration-2's own F1–F5 numbering to avoid confusion in the cumulative document.
